summary: Firecrawl typosquat fetches and executes unpinned binary from generic GitHub account
details: Package 'crw' impersonates the Firecrawl SDK: it declares 'firecrawl' in its keywords, replicates Firecrawl's public API (CrwClient with scrape/crawl/map/search), and routes its cloud mode to https://fastcrw.com/api (a Firecrawl-shaped domain). On first use of CrwClient.scrape/crawl/map or invocation of the crw-mcp CLI, src/crw/_binary.py queries https://api.github.com/repos/us/crw/releases/latest (a generic two-character GitHub handle that does not match any documented Firecrawl publisher), downloads the archive from https://github.com/us/crw/releases/download/v<version>/<asset>, extracts a 'crw-mcp' binary with no hash or signature verification, chmods +x, and runs it via os.execvp in __main__.py. The 'latest' tag is mutable, so whoever controls github.com/us/crw can change the executed bytes at any time. Combined with the typosquat framing, a developer who installs this thinking it is the Firecrawl SDK ends up executing arbitrary attacker-controlled code on first API call.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
